openers: لا تفوّتوا فرصتكم والعبوا معنا واحصلوا على فرصة الفوز بـ​ COUNTRYMAN​ 😍

dos: show iftar gatherings with families of multiple generations together
feature genuine traditional dishes (dates, qatayef, sambousa, harees) with Arabic naming
depict charity (zakat, gifting, food distribution) without exploiting the recipient
use warm golden-hour lighting and lantern-warm color palette in weeks 1-2
shift to celebratory-anticipatory tone in last 10 nights
respect prayer times: no posting during Maghreb prayer window (auto-quiet 18:00-18:30 AST during Ramadan)

sector_tensions.name: The meal that takes all day deserves to be seen
sector_tensions.territory: The gap between how much effort goes into food and how little that effort is visible to the person who eats it. For brands whose product requires skill, patience, or heritage technique.

sector_tensions.name: Real hospitality is never convenient
sector_tensions.territory: The tension between the Saudi culture of generous, unhurried hospitality and the modern pressure to make everything fast and efficient. Brands that take this tension seriously earn a position no fast-casual brand can own.

sector_tensions.name: The kitchen is the most honest room in the house
sector_tensions.territory: Food as a window into real life — the unpolished, unfiltered, real Saudi home. Not styled food photography but the truth of how food is actually made, shared, and eaten.

sector_tensions.name: Food memory is the strongest kind
sector_tensions.territory: The specific, sensory, personal memory of a food — its smell, the specific texture, the exact context. Brands that connect their product to food memory earn an emotional position that is nearly impossible to displace.

precedents.insight: Iftar is not a meal; it is a return. The audience emotion isn't hunger satisfied — it is family reassembled. Center the moment of reunion, not the food.
what_worked: Three-phase arc respects the holy month's emotional progression
Reunion framing differentiates from sector iftar-spread sameness
Authentic Saudi household details (hand-poured coffee, dates first) signal native-made
do_not_transplant: Reunion insight is specific to multi-generational extended-family brands — single-occupant audiences will not resonate
Requires 21-day lead time; cannot be done as a one-week tactical sprint

precedents.insight: Don't avoid the Ramadan-ad cliché — embrace it as a commentary. The campaign performs the genre while subverting it.
what_worked: Self-aware genre-play signals confidence and earns audience smile
Cultural commentary outperforms straight commercial content
Targets audience that's saturated with sector clichés
do_not_transplant: Requires audience saturated with the genre being parodied
Easy to misread as disrespectful; restraint critical for religious-occasion content

_source: DNA v3 + real hashtags + occasion_facts + strategy_frameworks + campaign_archive
